headlines.title: Supreme Court allows Trump to remove protections from thousands of Haitian and Syrian immigrants
headlines.description: WASHINGTON — The Supreme Court on Thursday cleared the way for the Trump administration to remove legal protections from thousands of Haitian and Syrian immigrants in the United States, meaning they could be subject to deportation

headlines.title: Trump to meet with Speaker Mike Johnson to discuss gridlock over key housing and election bills
headlines.description: President Donald Trump is expected to meet with House Speaker Mike Johnson, R-La., at the White House Thursday afternoon to discuss a path forward on the legislative impasse on Capitol Hill, a source familiar with the meeting said.

headlines.title: Timeline: The ups and downs of Trump’s Reflecting Pool renovation project
headlines.description: The Reflecting Pool on the National Mall was supposed to get a face-lift to celebrate America’s 250th birthday. Instead, it’s become a political talking point.

headlines.title: Supreme Court rules for Trump on asylum claims at the border
headlines.description: The Supreme Court backed the Trump administration’s bid to strengthen its ability to regulate the entry of asylum seekers at the U.S.-Mexico border.

headlines.title: Supreme Court allows Trump to end protected status for Haitian and Syrian immigrants
headlines.description: The Supreme Court has ruled to allow the Trump administration to end the Temporary Protected Status for thousands of immigrants from Haiti and Syria. NBC News' Gary Grumbach reports on the court's split among ideological lines and how the ruling impacts the administration's immigration policies.

headlines.title: Park Police Seeks to Identify Woman on Video at Reflecting Pool
headlines.description: The United States Park Police is looking for the public’s help in identifying a woman seen on camera near the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool with her hand in the water apparently pulling up an object. Several people have been arrested in recent days for allegedly vandalizing the historic landmark while it has been plagued with algae and peeling pain issues.

headlines.title: Senate Reverses Course on War Powers After Visit From Trump
headlines.description: In a major reversal, Senate Republicans refused to rein in President Donald Trump’s war powers, hours after he chastised members of his own party for going against him. Earlier in the day, the president refused to take part in a planned ceremony to sign a bill overwhelmingly passed by both parties aimed at making housing affordable, until, he says, the Senate passes the SAVE Act, which would require voters show proof of ID and citizenship documents to vote. NBC’s Kelly O’Donnell reports fo

headlines.title: Americans believe they aren’t being taught enough about democracy
headlines.description: For Thomas Jefferson, James Madison and other bold-face names struggling to bring about a new nation, an educated and informed citizenry was essential to the success of the American experiment.

headlines.title: State Assemblyman Alex Bores speaks on AI and politics
headlines.description: State Assemblyman Alex Bores, a candidate for New York's 12th Congressional District, speaks with NBC News’ Gadi Schwartz about why some top artificial intelligence companies are opposing his candidacy and whether AI should play a role in politics.

headlines.title: Riding primary momentum, democratic socialists eye a battleground state breakthrough
headlines.description: After a string of primary successes this month in deep-blue corners of the country, democratic socialists will face a major test of whether their appeal can translate to a battleground state later this summer.

headlines.title: Judge blocks Trump’s executive order on mail voting
headlines.description: A federal judge on Thursday blocked the implementation of key parts of President Donald Trump’s executive order on mail voting, declaring the plans to intervene in state-run elections unconstitutional.

headlines.title: Supreme Court strikes down Hawaii’s ‘vampire rule’ for gun owners
headlines.description: The Supreme Court struck down a Hawaii gun restriction that limits when people can carry firearms on certain private properties, known as the “vampire rule.”
